resources.string: Charging current information is not available on this device.
Prevent screen from sleeping during measurement
Toggle between Light and Dark themes
Charging current is the rate at which electrical energy is flowing into your device\'s battery, measured in amperes (A) or milliamperes (mA). A higher current means faster charging.

The application utilizes the system BatteryManager APIs to query the real-time electrical current (BATTERY_PROPERTY_CURRENT_NOW). We retrieve samples periodically according to your settings and display them in both Amperes (A) and Milliamperes (mA).

Different Android manufacturers implement battery hardware drivers differently. Some report current in microamperes (which we automatically convert to milliamperes), some report negative values when discharging (which we normalize), and a few older or budget devices do not support hardware charging sensors at all.

Average current is calculated dynamically as the sum of all sampled current values divided by the total number of samples collected since the beginning of the measurement session or since the last reset.
A utility designed to continuously measure, sample, and display the battery charging current. Built using standard BatteryManager APIs to assist in evaluating chargers and cable performance.
